trigger.action_pattern: ^(take .+ from .+|go to .+|open .+|close .+|use .+|move .+ to .+|put .+ in .+|put .+ on .+|heat .+ with .+|cool .+ with .+|clean .+ with .+|slice .+ with .+|examine .+)$
trigger.internal_state_condition: pddl_facts: primary entity name absent from all Proposition arguments (known_entities set derived from request_infos.facts=True)

rules.replacement_text_template: There is no '{entity}' in this game world. Check your admissible commands for valid entity names.

utility.evidence: Distinguishes 'entity never exists in this game' from 'entity exists but is inaccessible'; prevents the agent from exploring indefinitely for a fundamentally non-existent object.

novelty.evidence: admissible_commands reflects current reachable state only; an entity in a closed container is also absent from AC, so AC alone cannot confirm non-existence — only the full pddl_facts set can.

non_leakage.evidence: Confirming entity non-existence reveals nothing about task-relevant objects or solution path; entity names already appearing in admissible_commands already imply their existence.

semantic_preservation.evidence: Only the observation text is modified; score, done, admissible_commands, and all numeric/boolean fields are passed through unchanged.

testability.evidence: pddl_facts_state oracle can verify: referenced entity name appears in zero Proposition arguments across the full facts list at the step where the command failed.

trigger.internal_state_condition: pddl_facts: NOT holds(agent_1, *) — no holds proposition present

rules.replacement_text_template: You are not holding anything. You need to pick up an object before you can place it somewhere.

utility.evidence: Disambiguates 'move/put while hands empty' from all other Nothing happens causes; enables a correct pick-then-place recovery strategy without further inference.

novelty.evidence: admissible_commands does not expose inventory state; the agent cannot distinguish this failure from entity-nonexistent or wrong-location without consulting the holds fact separately.

non_leakage.evidence: Inventory state is already exposed via info['inventory']; reporting 'not holding anything' discloses no hidden game-world placement information.

semantic_preservation.evidence: Only the observation text is modified; score, done, admissible_commands, and all numeric/boolean fields are passed through unchanged.

testability.evidence: pddl_facts_state oracle can verify: holds(agent_1, *) absent from facts at the step where move/put failed; admissible_commands_validity_heuristic confirms command was not in AC.

rules.replacement_text_template: The {container} is closed. You need to open it first before you can take anything from it.

utility.evidence: Closed-container and wrong-location failures require completely different recovery actions (open vs. explore); the augmentation removes that ambiguity directly.

novelty.evidence: Although 'open X' appears in AC when X is closed, the agent must parse AC and connect that to the failed take command; augmentation makes the causal link explicit without requiring multi-step AC reasoning.

non_leakage.evidence: Container name comes directly from the agent's own command token; closedness (openable AND NOT opened) is observable world state, not hidden solution information.

semantic_preservation.evidence: Only the observation text is modified; score, done, admissible_commands, and all numeric/boolean fields are passed through unchanged.

testability.evidence: pddl_facts_state oracle can verify: openable(Y) AND NOT opened(Y) in facts at the step where take X from Y failed.

trigger.internal_state_condition: pddl_facts: inreceptacle(<object>, <other_receptacle>) where <other_receptacle> != <receptacle_in_command>; AND receptacle in command is NOT closed (R03 guard has already failed)

rules.replacement_text_template: You cannot find {object} in the {container}. That object is not there. Try looking around other locations.

utility.evidence: Wrong-location failure requires exploration of other receptacles; disambiguating it from closed-container prevents wasted open-then-take attempts on the wrong container.

novelty.evidence: AC may or may not contain 'take X from Z' depending on whether Z is accessible; augmentation reliably indicates wrong-receptacle failure without requiring exhaustive AC scan across all possible take commands.

non_leakage.evidence: Feedback reports only that the object is absent from the stated receptacle ('not there, try elsewhere'); the actual location Z derived from inreceptacle(X, Z) is never disclosed.

semantic_preservation.evidence: Only the observation text is modified; score, done, admissible_commands, and all numeric/boolean fields are passed through unchanged.

testability.evidence: pddl_facts_state oracle can verify: inreceptacle(X, Z) for Z != Y in facts at the step where take X from Y failed, and Y is not closed (R03 guard excludes closed-container case).

trigger.internal_state_condition: pddl_facts: NOT holds(agent_1, <object>) — agent holds nothing OR holds a different object than the one named in the command

rules.replacement_text_template: You are not holding {object}. You need to pick up {object} before you can {verb} it.

utility.evidence: Distinguishes 'heat/cool/clean X while not holding X' from entity-nonexistent failure; enables a correct pick-then-operate recovery without guessing at the cause.

novelty.evidence: Although info['inventory'] is available, the causal link between a failed heat/cool/clean command and the missing holds(agent_1, X) precondition is not spelled out in any existing feedback text.

non_leakage.evidence: Inventory state is already exposed via info['inventory']; reporting 'you are not holding X' discloses no hidden game-world placement or task-solution information.

semantic_preservation.evidence: Only the observation text is modified; score, done, admissible_commands, and all numeric/boolean fields are passed through unchanged.

testability.evidence: pddl_facts_state oracle can verify: holds(agent_1, X) absent from facts, or holds(agent_1, Y) present with Y != X, at the step where heat/cool/clean X with appliance failed.

rules.replacement_text_template: That command is not recognized. Valid actions include: go to, take, move, open, close, examine, use, heat, cool, clean. Type 'help' to see all commands.

utility.evidence: Distinguishes syntactically unrecognized verb from all other Nothing happens causes; prevents the agent from incorrectly inferring inventory or entity issues from a parse-level failure.

novelty.evidence: admissible_commands shows valid command strings but does not expose a verb-is-unrecognized diagnostic; the agent must scan all AC entries to infer the verb class is absent, which is expensive and error-prone.

non_leakage.evidence: Reporting 'command format unrecognized' reveals no game-state information; it only restates that the command failed at the parser layer before any world-state lookup occurs.

semantic_preservation.evidence: Only the observation text is modified; score, done, admissible_commands, and all numeric/boolean fields are passed through unchanged.

testability.evidence: admissible_commands_validity_heuristic oracle can verify: command verb is absent from all entries in admissible_commands while the observation changes to the new diagnostic form.

dropped_candidates.reason: Fails testability (verdict was hard_to_test): the PDDL mechanism gating examine-admissibility on toggled(desklamp) vs. wrong-location is unresolved. All 37 observed instances had was_admissible=false, but the probing agent was typically NOT at the target object's location, making examine_without_lamp_on and wrong-location causes indistinguishable without a targeted probe. Also fails non_leakage (verdict was needs_review): reporting 'a light source must be on' may implicitly reveal task-type structure for look_at_obj_in_light tasks if the intro observation has not already stated this requirement. Both criteria cannot be resolved without a confirmed PDDL-level causal probe.

Fails utility (verdict was unclear): whether a step-limit terminal observation improves RL training depends on training-loop design, and no empirical evidence supports the benefit. Fails testability (verdict was hard_to_test): no trajectory in the probing catalog reached max_episode_steps (observed_frequency=0); behavior is inferred from spec reading and Limit wrapper source, not from live observation. The proposed hook (intercepting done=True AND NOT won=True) is also structurally different from all other text augmentation rules and requires a separate interception point in the step() return path.

wrapper_invariants: no new info keys except under namespaced prefix (progress_*)
